fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: Trace to logs
fields.description: Navigate from a trace span to the selected data source's logs.

help.markdown:
Navigate from a Zipkin trace span to the selected logs data source. Written by `@grafana/o11y-ds-frontend`'s `TraceToLogsSection` (v2 shape); the editor clears the legacy `tracesToLogs` key on any write.

Shape: `{ datasourceUid?: string, tags?: Array<{ key: string, value?: string }>, spanStartTimeShift?: string, spanEndTimeShift?: string, filterByTraceID?: boolean, filterBySpanID?: boolean, query?: string, customQuery: boolean }`.

- `datasourceUid` — target logs datasource UID (loki, elasticsearch, splunk, opensearch, falconlogscale, googlecloud-logging, victoriametrics-logs).
- `tags` — key/value tag mappings used to build the log query.
- `spanStartTimeShift` / `spanEndTimeShift` — time-range shifts applied when jumping to logs (e.g. `-1m`, `2m`).
- `filterByTraceID` / `filterBySpanID` — filter the logs by the trace/span ID; disabled when `customQuery` is on.
- `customQuery` — use a custom query template; when true, `query` is used with `$__tags` interpolation.

Docs: [Trace to logs](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/zipkin/configure-zipkin-data-source/#trace-to-logs).

fields.description: Legacy v1 shape for Trace to logs. `getTraceToLogsOptions` (packages/grafana-o11y-ds-frontend/src/TraceToLogs/TraceToLogsSettings.tsx) migrates this to `tracesToLogsV2` on read; the editor clears this key whenever `tracesToLogsV2` is written. Retained here for round-trip compatibility of legacy datasources.

fields.label: Trace to metrics
fields.description: Navigate from a trace span to the selected data source's metrics.

help.markdown:
Navigate from a Zipkin trace span to the selected metrics data source (Prometheus or victoriametrics-metrics). Written by `@grafana/o11y-ds-frontend`'s `TraceToMetricsSection`.

Shape: `{ datasourceUid?: string, tags?: Array<{ key: string, value: string }>, queries: Array<{ name?: string, query?: string }>, spanStartTimeShift?: string, spanEndTimeShift?: string }`.

- `datasourceUid` — target metrics datasource UID.
- `tags` — key/value tag mappings used in the metrics query.
- `queries` — list of Prometheus queries offered on the trace→metrics link, each with a display name and a query template that interpolates `$__tags`.
- `spanStartTimeShift` / `spanEndTimeShift` — time-range shifts (default placeholders `-2m` / `2m`).

Docs: [Trace to metrics](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/zipkin/configure-zipkin-data-source/#trace-to-metrics).

fields.label: Node graph
fields.description: Show or hide the node graph visualization.

help.markdown:
Display the node graph above the trace view. Written by `@grafana/o11y-ds-frontend`'s `NodeGraphSection`.

Shape: `{ enabled?: boolean }`. Default: disabled.

Read by the Zipkin frontend datasource (`src/datasource.ts:34,55`): when enabled, the client-side query pipeline appends node-graph frames to the response.

Docs: [Node graph](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/zipkin/configure-zipkin-data-source/#node-graph).

fields.label: Span bar
fields.description: Add additional info next to the service and operation on a span bar row in the trace view.

help.markdown:
Additional label rendered next to service/operation on each span bar row in the trace view. Written by `@grafana/o11y-ds-frontend`'s `SpanBarSection`.

Shape: `{ type?: 'None' | 'Duration' | 'Tag', tag?: string }`.

- `type` — label source. `'None'` hides the extra label; `'Duration'` (default, shown as placeholder in the editor) renders the span duration; `'Tag'` renders the value of the tag named by `tag`.
- `tag` — required when `type` is `'Tag'`; the tag key whose value is displayed (searched in span attributes and resource attributes).

Docs: [Span bar](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/zipkin/configure-zipkin-data-source/#span-bar).

groups.title: Additional settings
groups.description: Additional settings are optional settings that can be configured for more control over your data source.

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.tsx.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
tracesToLogs (v1) is legacy; the editor migrates it to tracesToLogsV2 on read (getTraceToLogsOptions) and clears it on any write to tracesToLogsV2. Provisioning payloads should prefer tracesToLogsV2.

instructions.msg: Zipkin authentication is built on Grafana's HTTP settings model, not a discriminator field. The editor's three visible auth methods write these flags: 'Basic authentication' sets root.basicAuth=true and provides root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' sets jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' leaves both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — the editor's onAuthMethodSelect (@grafana/plugin-ui utils.ts) always writes both. The Zipkin backend delegates HTTP client construction entirely to settings.HTTPClientOptions (pkg/zipkin/zipkin.go:23), which honours basicAuth, tlsAuth, tlsAuthWithCACert, tlsSkipVerify, serverName, timeout, keepCookies, and OAuth pass-through automatically.

Minimal Basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9411","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal No-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9411","jsonData":{}}. Minimal OAuth-forward payload: {"url":"http://localhost:9411","jsonData":{"oauthPassThru":true}}. root.url is required — the Zipkin backend rejects an empty URL (pkg/zipkin/zipkin.go:33-35) and issues HTTP requests against '<url>/api/v2/services' (health check / services, pkg/zipkin/client.go:48-68), '<url>/api/v2/spans' (spans by service, client.go:72-98), '<url>/api/v2/traces' (traces search, client.go:102-130), and '<url>/api/v2/trace/{traceId}' (trace by ID, client.go:134-173).

Trace-to-X mappings live under two jsonData keys: tracesToLogsV2 (v2 shape written by @grafana/o11y-ds-frontend's TraceToLogsSection — the editor clears the legacy tracesToLogs key on any write; getTraceToLogsOptions migrates old→new on read) and tracesToMetrics (single shape, may contain multiple named queries). Zipkin does NOT expose Trace-to-profiles or a service-graph section, unlike Tempo, and has no Trace-by-ID time-parameters toggle, unlike Jaeger. Each carries a datasourceUid targeting a specific plugin type: tracesToLogsV2 → loki / elasticsearch / grafana-splunk-datasource / grafana-opensearch-datasource / grafana-falconlogscale-datasource / googlecloud-logging-datasource / victoriametrics-logs-datasource; tracesToMetrics → prometheus / victoriametrics-metrics-datasource. Provisioning payloads should write tracesToLogsV2 directly and omit tracesToLogs.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. Custom HTTP header values follow the same pattern via httpHeaderValue<N> secrets — they are modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders field (indexedPair storage): @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component writes indexed httpHeaderName<N> (jsonData) / httpHeaderValue<N> (secureJsonData) pairs starting at index 1.

Frontend-only Zipkin knobs: jsonData.nodeGraph.enabled toggles the node graph above the trace view (also read by the frontend datasource at src/datasource.ts:34,55 to attach node-graph frames on the client side); jsonData.spanBar.type ('None'|'Duration'|'Tag') + jsonData.spanBar.tag decorate span rows; jsonData.tracesToLogsV2 / jsonData.tracesToMetrics are consumed by the trace view and query editor. None of these are read by the Zipkin Go backend — the backend only reads root.url (required) and the SDK-managed HTTP settings. The Zipkin plugin ships no pkg/models/settings.go and no typed backend jsonData contract.

Zipkin URL points at the Zipkin v2 HTTP API root (default placeholder http://localhost:9411). The backend appends '/api/v2/services', '/api/v2/spans', '/api/v2/traces', and '/api/v2/trace/{traceId}' when calling the server; provisioning payloads should therefore pass the API root, NOT a path that already contains '/api/v2'. url.JoinPath (pkg/zipkin/client.go:50,140,183) handles trailing slashes but not accidental double-'/api/v2' segments.
